title: U.S. Sanctions Pause Adds Political Win to Russia’s Economic Gain From Iran War
summary: Kremlin officials said the American move, which Europe opposes, showed that Moscow could not be dislodged from the center of global energy markets.

title: Consumer Prices Rose in January, Before Iran War Added Price Pressures
summary: Economic growth at the end of 2025 was revised downward and consumer prices rose at the start of 2026.

title: How This Oil Supply Shock Compares With the Embargo of 1973
summary: Governments have stockpiled oil, and cars are more efficient but the supply shock is global, and there’s no sense of when it’ll end.

title: Wall Street Bankers Offered Lucrative Access to Join the Pentagon
summary: A presentation from a headhunting firm aimed to recruit Wall Street investors to the Pentagon by offering “unmatched access” to government officials and fund-raising opportunities among foreign sovereigns.

title: Pete Hegseth Says ‘the Sooner David Ellison’ Buys CNN, ‘the Better’
summary: The remarks fueled concerns that CNN’s prospective new owner may shift its coverage in a Trump-friendly direction.

title: Cuba Acknowledges Talks with Trump Officials For the First Time
summary: President Miguel Díaz-Canel, whose country is rapidly running out of fuel, said the talks were based on “respect for the political systems of both countries.”

title: After Synagogue Attack in Michigan, Some Jews Wonder How Much More Security Is Possible
summary: “We are synagogues — we are houses of worship,” one rabbi said. “We are not Fort Knox.”

title: What We Know About Michigan Synagogue Attack on Temple Israel
summary: The attacker who drove into Temple Israel in West Bloomfield Township, a 41-year-old naturalized U.S. citizen, is dead. No one else was killed.

title: His Harvard Lab Was Thriving. Then Came the Cuts.
summary: Will Mair, who studies aging, lost almost all his research funds when the White House cracked down on Harvard. He was wholly unprepared for the upheaval that followed.

title: Behind the Scenes of the 2026 Oscar Best Picture Nominees: ‘Sinners,’ ‘Hamnet’ and More
summary: In these videos, directors walked us through pivotal sequences from their 2026 Academy Award-nominated films.

title: When a President Gets Addicted to Regime Change
summary: Venezuela gave Trump a taste of success. This isn’t the first time an American president has gotten hooked on overthrowing foreign governments.

title: Sam Page Poised to Beat Phil Berger in High Stakes N.C. Primary
summary: A formal tally on Friday showed Sam Page ahead of Phil Berger, the powerful longtime leader of the State Senate. A recount is expected but experts say that is unlikely to flip the results.

title: At Dinners, Over Jokes With Comedians, Epstein Honed His Networking
summary: In the years after his 2008 conviction for soliciting prostitution, Jeffrey Epstein rebuilt his reputation by hosting gatherings with leaders in all sorts of trades, including comedy.

title: Homes in This California Enclave Come With a Catch: Living on a Landslide Complex
summary: The soil keeps shifting in Rancho Palos Verdes, Calif. For the right buyer, that presents a great opportunity, at least for the time being.

title: Palisades Fire Recovery Tests L.A.’s Ability to Invest in Resilience
summary: Palisades fire victims want to raise money for disaster hardening. Their idea could be a model — if it can get past L.A.’s most vexing housing problems.

title: A New Lifeline Helps Inmates Transition to Life Outside the Bars
summary: Medicaid is now paying for health care in jails and prisons, helping smooth inmates’ return to the community. Corrections and law enforcement officials say they’re all for it.

title: John F. Burns, Prize-winning Foreign Correspondent for The Times, Dies at 81
summary: In a 40-year career that brought him two Pulitzers, he reported from trouble spots around the world, eloquently conveying the chaos of war.

title: A.I. Chatbots Want Your Health Records. Tread Carefully.
summary: Following rivals like Amazon and OpenAI, Microsoft is upgrading its artificially intelligent assistant to track your health. There are benefits and risks to consider.

title: The Death Penalty Is Indefensible. It’s Also on the Rise.
summary: The surge in capital punishment is a cruel and unjust development.

title: Mussolini Would Have Loved Trump’s Ballroom
summary: Trump’s plans for Washington bring to mind what Mussolini did — and tried to do — to Rome.

title: A Dangerous Bottleneck
summary: Iran is turning the Strait of Hormuz into a battlefield, proving that a short and surgical war with Iran could be a fantasy.

title: How Do You Feel About Traveling Right Now? We Want to Know.
summary: With spring and summer travel season beginning amid a war in the Middle East, a partial government shutdown and more, we’d like to hear how your travel plans are changing.

title: Phones ‘Ringing Off the Hook’ for Ukraine Defense Firms as Mideast Seeks Help
summary: Ukraine wants to leverage its defense expertise into security partnerships and to reap potentially vast profits for its arms industry.

title: To Secure BTS Concert Tickets, K-Pop Fans Crowd Internet Cafes in South Korea
summary: The K-pop supergroup’s upcoming reunion concert prompted a rush for the cafes, which offer solid connections and a sense of community.

title: Former Green Beret Behind a Failed Coup in Venezuela Is on the Run
summary: The U.S. government said the military veteran, Jordan G. Goudreau, had been missing for months, and that an ankle monitor assigned to him had been found hidden in a piece of furniture.
